rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unsafe copy at program.c:11 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained that user-controlled argv data can exceed buf[256], causing a global buffer overflow. They explicitly localized the issue to that copy and described the overflow condition.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed strncpy with explicit null-termination but did not reject overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejecting inputs that exceed the destination size, which was not included.
rationale.global_fix: They suggested using std::string or a custom bounded type without a concrete, applicable bounded C API or centralized argv validation plan. This lacks explicit, actionable steps to remove unbounded string handling in this C program.
